source: Incremental Learning for Matrix Factorization in Recommender Systems

concepts.finding: Incremental MF for streaming recommendation data. New user/item ratings processed incrementally without full retraining. Techniques: (1) alternating least squares on new data only, (2) warm-start with existing factors, (3) forgetting factor for temporal dynamics. 100-1000x faster than full retraining with <1% accuracy loss.
concepts.implication: Incremental keyword embedding updates as new co-occurrence data arrives. New keyword-intent relationships processed without full retraining. Forgetting factor handles temporal keyword shifts (seasonal intents).
concepts.open_question: How often should keyword embeddings be incrementally updated vs. fully retrained? What forgetting factor captures keyword intent evolution?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Incremental keyword embedding updates (100x faster than full retraining) maintain >95% of intent cluster quality for up to 30 days between full retraining cycles.
